name: First Federal Savings And Loan Association
description_short: Community savings and loan association in Hazard, Kentucky offering residential mortgages, deposit accounts, CDs, and IRAs since 1960.
description_long:
First Federal Savings and Loan Association of Hazard has operated as a locally-focused financial institution in eastern Kentucky for over 60 years, establishing itself as a community-based provider of banking and lending services. The institution serves individuals and families seeking traditional banking products with a personal touch, emphasizing long-term customer relationships in a small-town setting.

The company offers a full spectrum of deposit products including passbook savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), and individual retirement accounts (IRAs). On the lending side, their portfolio encompasses one- to four-family residential mortgages, construction loans, multi-family property mortgages, nonresidential commercial loans, and consumer loans secured by deposits or CDs. They position themselves as home loan specialists with dedicated mortgage expertise.

What distinguishes First Federal is their emphasis on personal service and local accessibility. They highlight "friendly faces you've come to trust" and brand themselves as "Your Hometown Friends. Your Hometown Bank." The physical branch offers modern conveniences including a drive-through window, ATM access, and safety deposit boxes, with extended Saturday hours (9 AM-Noon drive-thru only) for working customers. Their staff provides advisory services on retirement planning and mortgage products.

As a traditional savings and loan association, First Federal operates with limited geographic footprint (single branch in Hazard, KY) and may have fewer digital banking conveniences compared to larger institutions. They are best suited for local Hazard-area residents and those seeking relationship-based banking rather than national reach or cutting-edge fintech features. Their long operational history and focus on residential lending suggest stability, though as a smaller institution they may have less competitive rates or product diversity than major banks. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Consumer loans (secured by savings deposits or CDs)
pros: Established 1960 with 60+ year history demonstrates institutional stability
Specialized home loan expertise with dedicated mortgage staff available for consultation
Saturday drive-through hours (9 AM-Noon) provide weekend banking access
Physical ATM on-site for convenient cash access
Safety deposit boxes available for document and valuables storage
Full IRA products offered with personalized advisory services
Local, relationship-based banking with same staff members over time
cons: Single branch location limits accessibility to Hazard, KY area residents only
No mention of online banking, mobile app, or digital services on website
Limited business hours (closes 4 PM weekdays) may be inconvenient for working customers
As smaller institution, likely cannot compete on rates with national banks
No mention of checking accounts despite being core banking service
best_for: Hazard, Kentucky area residents seeking local relationship banking
First-time homebuyers wanting personalized mortgage guidance
Individuals opening IRAs and seeking retirement planning advice
Small business owners and commercial property investors in Perry County
diagnosis: First Federal is best for existing or prospective residents of Hazard, Kentucky seeking straightforward banking services and mortgage expertise from a stable, locally-owned institution with personal service. The main caveat is geographic limitation to a single branch and apparent lack of digital banking options, making this unsuitable for customers requiring online access, mobile banking, or service outside the immediate Hazard area.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
